title: Enhance fake hacking app with more dramatic effects

requirements.summary: Add animated hacking progress bar to Terminal page that triggers on 'hack' command with multi-stage progress sequence and dramatic completion
acceptanceCriteria: Running the 'hack' command in the terminal triggers a visible animated progress bar
Progress bar advances through at least 3 labeled stages with realistic hacker-themed labels
Each stage completes with a short delay to simulate real processing
A dramatic success or failure message is displayed upon completion
Progress bar matches the existing terminal green color theme and monospace font styling

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that manages the hacking progress animation state, tracking the current stage index, progress percentage, stage labels, and completion status. The hook should expose methods to start, advance, and complete the progress sequence, along with the current stage data for rendering.

Create a React component that renders an animated progress bar with stage labels for the hacking sequence. The component should display the current stage label (e.g., 'Bypassing firewall...', 'Injecting payload...', 'Extracting data...'), a visual progress indicator using terminal-green colors matching the existing theme, and a completion message. Use monospace font and OKLCH color variables from the existing theme (--terminal-green, --terminal-green-bright). Include smooth CSS transitions for progress bar width changes.

Modify the command processor to detect the 'hack' command and trigger the hacking progress sequence instead of returning static text. Update the command handler to return a special flag or state indicating that a progress animation should be displayed, allowing the Terminal component to render the HackProgressBar component when this command is executed.

Integrate the HackProgressBar component into the Terminal page to display it when the 'hack' command is executed. Add conditional rendering logic to show the progress bar during the hacking sequence, hide the input field while the animation is active, and append the completion message to the terminal output history when the sequence finishes. Ensure the progress bar appears below the existing terminal output and above the command input area.

requirements.summary: Add typewriter effect to Terminal page command responses so output appears character-by-character or line-by-line for dramatic, authentic feel
acceptanceCriteria: Command responses in the terminal appear with a typewriter/streaming animation instead of instantly
Animation speed is fast enough to feel snappy but slow enough to be visually readable
Multiple simultaneous output lines animate sequentially
Existing terminal commands (help, scan, trace, decrypt, exploit, etc.) all use the animated output
Animation does not block user from typing the next command after it finishes

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that implements a typewriter effect for animating text output. The hook should accept text or an array of lines, a typing speed parameter, and return the currently visible text along with an isComplete flag. Use requestAnimationFrame or setInterval to progressively reveal characters or lines at the specified speed, and clean up timers on unmount or when the animation completes.

Create a React component that renders animated typewriter text using the useTypewriter hook. The component should accept output text (single line or array of lines) and color props, display the text with a typewriter animation effect, and emit an onComplete callback when the animation finishes. Use the monospace font and maintain the existing terminal styling.

Integrate the TypewriterOutput component into the terminal output rendering logic. Replace the static output line rendering with the TypewriterOutput component for command responses. Manage a queue of pending outputs to ensure multiple lines animate sequentially. Store the fully-animated lines in terminal history so they remain visible after animation completes. Ensure the command input remains available and does not block after each animation finishes.

Update the boot script hook to work with the new typewriter effect by returning lines in a format compatible with TypewriterOutput, or by adding a flag indicating whether each line should be animated. Ensure the boot sequence uses the typewriter effect for a consistent dramatic experience when the Terminal page first loads.
